ev.headline: Hyundai launches first China-made EV overseas to compete with Tesla and BYD
ev.summary: Hyundai is bringing its first dedicated EV for China, the Elexio, to overseas markets including Australia, positioned as an alternative to Tesla Model Y with competitive pricing.

ev.headline: Lithium metal giant begins semi-solid-state EV battery production
ev.summary: Ganfeng Lithium has begun mass-producing semi-solid-state batteries with 650 Wh/kg energy density, with supply deals to Tesla, Volkswagen, Hyundai and other major OEMs for next-generation EV batteries.

ev.headline: Volvo recalls 40,000 EX30 electric SUVs due to fire risk
ev.summary: Volvo is recalling more than 40,000 EX30 electric SUVs due to a potential fire risk, affecting a significant portion of this popular EV model.

ev.headline: Stellantis takes $26B write-down over EVs, sells stake in battery JV
ev.summary: Stellantis announced a $26.2 billion write-down over EV strategy shifts and sold its 49% stake in NextStar Energy battery venture to LG Energy Solution for $100, reflecting massive losses from overestimating EV demand.

ev.headline: Polestar details plan for next four EV model launches through 2028
ev.summary: Polestar announced four new vehicle launches including the Polestar 5, new Polestar 4 variant, next-generation Polestar 2, and compact premium Polestar 7 SUV by 2028, with expectations for low double-digit growth in 2026.

ev.headline: Mercedes-AMG GT XX performance EV to debut by May 2026 with 1,340 HP
ev.summary: Mercedes-Benz confirms its Mercedes-AMG GT XX electric performance car will debut by May 2026 with over 1,000 kW power, tri-motor AWD, and 250-mile charging capability in just 5 minutes.

ev.headline: Ford unveils $30,000 electric pickup strategy with UEV platform
ev.summary: Ford announced plans to launch a mid-size electric pickup around 2027 starting at $30,000 using a new Universal Electric Vehicle platform designed to reduce costs through efficiency and smaller batteries.

ev.headline: GM takes $7.2B charge on EV strategy shift due to declining demand
ev.summary: General Motors announced a $7.2 billion special charge related to realignment of its electric vehicle strategy following declining consumer demand and policy changes.

ev.headline: EV sales fell as hybrid vehicle sales continued to rise in 2025
ev.summary: Battery electric vehicle market share fell to less than 6% in Q4 2025 after peaking at 12% in September when tax credits expired, while hybrid vehicles gained increasing market share.

ev.headline: Massachusetts launches free bi-directional EV charging program
ev.summary: Massachusetts Clean Energy Center announced first-of-its-kind Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) demonstration program installing free bi-directional EV chargers for school districts, municipalities, and residents by summer 2026.

ev.headline: ChargePoint adds 190,000 EV chargers but still not keeping up with demand
ev.summary: ChargePoint reported adding 190,000 EV chargers in 2025, yet the growth still fails to keep pace with increasing demand from EV drivers.

ev.headline: The Mobility House launches Cascade EV Aggregator for vehicle-to-grid integration
ev.summary: The Mobility House North America unveiled Cascade EV Aggregator, a comprehensive V2G platform enabling utilities to coordinate EV charging and discharging across residential and fleet assets in the US and Canada.

ev.headline: McKinsey: EV battery recycling will be $70B annual business by 2035
ev.summary: McKinsey analysis projects EV battery recycling will become a $70 billion annual business by the end of the next decade as more EVs reach end-of-life.

ev.headline: Major automakers have written off $55B after overestimating EV demand
ev.summary: Stellantis, Ford, GM, and other major automakers have collectively written off approximately $55 billion after overestimating EV adoption rates and facing slower-than-expected market growth.

ev.headline: Trump administration resets fuel economy standards, eliminating EV incentives push
ev.summary: Trump administration unveiled 'Freedom Means Affordable Cars' proposal resetting CAFE standards to 34.5 mpg by 2031, reversing Biden-era EV mandates and eliminating regulatory pressure for electrification.

ev.headline: States continue EV support despite federal policy reversals
ev.summary: Environmental Defense Fund report shows all 50 US states have implemented EV-supportive policies despite federal rollbacks, with California leading with all 16 key policies in place.

ev.headline: Nissan Leaf gets new generation with 530 km WLTP range mid-2026
ev.summary: Nissan's third-generation Leaf, launching mid-2026, will feature the modern CMF-EV platform from the Ariya, delivering nearly double the range of the previous model at 530 km WLTP.

ev.headline: BMW iX3 2026 launches with 800+ km range and 400 kW charging
ev.summary: BMW's new 2026 iX3 based on Neue Klasse platform will offer over 800 km WLTP range, ultra-fast 400 kW charging, and 30% improved energy density in its batteries.

ev.headline: Texas battery storage installations hit record 57.6 GWh in 2025
ev.summary: US battery storage installations reached a record 57.6 GWh in 2025, up 30% year-over-year, with Texas set to overtake California as the leading state for battery storage capacity.

ev.headline: 2025 second-best year for US EV sales despite federal incentive loss
ev.summary: US EV sales declined only 4% in 2025 despite loss of federal tax credits, making it the second-best year on record as alternative incentives and lower prices drove continued adoption.

ev.headline: Kia EV4 GT opens pre-orders with electric hot hatch performance
ev.summary: Kia opened pre-orders for its EV4 GT, an electric hot hatch variant combining compact urban design with performance capabilities in the growing compact EV segment.

ev.headline: Toyota Hilux electric pickup to launch late 2026 in UK market
ev.summary: Toyota is launching an electric version of its Hilux pickup truck late 2026, offering an alternative to traditional combustion and hydrogen fuel cell variants.

ev.headline: Lamborghini scraps plans for Lanzador electric supercar first EV
ev.summary: Lamborghini has canceled its plans to build the Lanzador electric supercar, abandoning its first all-electric vehicle project.

ev.headline: BYD dominates Mexico EV market with 80% share in 2024
ev.summary: Chinese EV maker BYD captured 80% of Mexico's EV market in 2024 and nearly 90% by December 2025, accounting for approximately two-thirds of all EVs sold in the country.

energy.headline: Experts Predict Top Trends in U.S. Residential Solar for 2026
energy.summary: Industry experts forecast that solar installers will diversify into comprehensive home energy providers offering HVAC, storage, and smart home tech, while third-party ownership and virtual power plants continue to grow in importance.

energy.headline: The 30% Solar Tax Credit is Dead — Long Live the 30% Solar Tax Credit
energy.summary: Following the expiration of the 25D residential solar tax credit on December 31, 2025, homeowners can still access 30% incentives through Section 48E via third-party ownership models like leases and PPAs through 2027.

energy.headline: Trump and the Fate of the 30% Solar Tax Credit
energy.summary: Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill officially ended the 25D federal solar tax credit for homeowners on December 31, 2025, but homeowners can continue accessing solar incentives through third-party ownership arrangements through 2027.

energy.headline: President Trump Signs Bill Killing The Solar Tax Credit
energy.summary: President Trump signed legislation cutting the 30% residential solar tax credit nearly a decade ahead of schedule, but solar prices continue to decline with costs hitting record lows of $2.50 per watt.

energy.headline: Residential Solar Tax Credit Going Away In US After 2025
energy.summary: The 30% residential solar tax credit expires December 31, 2025, with no phase-down period, making 2025 critical for scheduling and installation to avoid missing the deadline.

energy.headline: What Solar Incentives Were Curtailed or Canceled?
energy.summary: The Trump administration's One Big Beautiful Bill ends residential solar and home electrification tax credits before 2027, with tighter commercial solar criteria and new foreign-entity constraints affecting projects.

energy.headline: With Tax Credits Expiring, Home Electrification Nonprofit is Racing Against the Clock
energy.summary: The 25D and 25C federal tax credits for solar, batteries, and home efficiency improvements expire at year-end 2025, prompting nonprofits like Rewiring America to urge homeowners to act quickly.

energy.headline: 2026-2027 U.S. Solar & HVAC Incentives Post-Federal Credit Era
energy.summary: State and utility incentives will increasingly replace federal credits, with programs like California's revised SGIP and Texas's HOMES/HEAR rebates reshaping solar and electrification financing.

energy.headline: California Residents Increasingly Pairing Battery Storage with Solar Installations
energy.summary: In California, over 50% of new residential solar installations were paired with battery storage by April 2024, up from 20% in October 2023, driven by changes to net metering policies.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs A Home Battery System: Which Is More Advantageous for Solar Owners?
energy.summary: As states phase out retail-rate net metering in favor of net billing, home batteries are gaining attention as prices decline and state incentives like California's SGIP help reduce upfront costs.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries in 2026: Which Saves You More on Solar?
energy.summary: States like California and Arizona have replaced full-retail net metering with lower-compensation net billing structures, making battery storage increasingly attractive despite higher upfront costs.

energy.headline: How Rooftop Solar and Net Metering Works
energy.summary: Net metering allows rooftop solar owners to sell excess power to utilities and receive credits on their bills, though not all states offer this incentive program.

energy.headline: Utah Gigasite Data Center Contemplates Solar-Storage Baseload Addition
energy.summary: Zeo Energy is studying development of 280 MW of solar and storage generation for a multi-gigawatt data center campus in Utah, with potential for thermal, chemical, or electrochemical long-duration storage.

energy.headline: FranklinWH Achieves Global Benchmark in Home Energy Reliability
energy.summary: FranklinWH's residential battery system became the first to earn TIA-942 Rated 1-4 certification, achieving data-center-grade reliability standards for home energy storage.

energy.headline: Virtual Power Plants Showed Up for Their Biggest Test Yet
energy.summary: Over 100,000 California homes in Sunrun's virtual power plant test dispatched 535 megawatts to the grid in July 2025, equivalent to half San Francisco's peak demand, demonstrating VPP potential.

energy.headline: Puerto Rico's Solar Revolution Powers Grid with Battery Storage
energy.summary: 175,000 Puerto Rican homes now have residential solar with 160,000 equipped with battery storage, dispatching 48 MW during peak demand events to keep the grid stable.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Pilot Provides Free Heat Pumps and Solar with $150 Monthly Savings
energy.summary: A Massachusetts program provided solar panels to 55 low-income households on Cape Cod and Martha's Vineyard, resulting in average monthly energy savings of $150 combined with heat pumps.

energy.headline: How Do Solar Rebates and Incentives Work in 2026?
energy.summary: Federal residential solar tax credits expired December 31, 2025, shifting focus to state and utility incentives through tax credits, property tax abatements, and performance-based rebates.

energy.headline: Net Metering – SEIA
energy.summary: 34 states plus Washington D.C. and Puerto Rico have mandatory net metering rules, which credit solar system owners for electricity exported to the grid and employ over 230,000 American workers.

energy.headline: Residential Clean Energy Credit Tax Requirements
energy.summary: The 30% federal residential clean energy tax credit applies to solar, battery storage, and geothermal systems installed through December 31, 2025, with specific qualification requirements.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries: What's Best for You?
energy.summary: While net metering avoids upfront battery costs, battery storage provides energy independence, backup power during outages, and potentially higher long-term savings in avoided peak-rate charges.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils New IQ Bidirectional EV Charger Architecture for Home Backup and Grid Services
v2x.summary: Enphase launched its IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G capability, designed with AI-powered energy management and expected availability in H2 2026.

v2x.headline: Toyota's Vehicle-to-Grid Program with Oncor Energy Demonstrates Benefits of Bidirectional BEV Charging
v2x.summary: Toyota advanced its V2G pilot with Oncor, demonstrating how bidirectional energy flow enhances grid stability and allows customers to reduce electricity costs during peak demand periods.

v2x.headline: Nissan to Launch Affordable Vehicle-to-Grid Technology in 2026
v2x.summary: Nissan announced plans to launch AC-based V2H and DC-based V2G solutions in 2026, aiming to make bidirectional charging accessible at comparable prices to mono-directional chargers.

v2x.headline: Nio Participates in Vehicle-to-Grid Demonstration Discharge with Over 100 EVs
v2x.summary: More than 100 Nio vehicles participated in a V2G discharge demonstration in Tianjin, showcasing how EVs can supply electricity to the grid during peak demand.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Grid Market Set to Reach US$ 65.84 Billion by 2035
v2x.summary: Market analysis projects V2G sector will reach $65.84 billion by 2035 as regulatory harmonization and commercial-scale virtual power plants accelerate adoption globally.

v2x.headline: Retired EV Batteries Scored a New Gig: Bolstering Texas' Grid
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions is repurposing retired GM EV batteries to provide grid storage in Texas, offering cheaper power to infrastructure developers and supporting grid reliability.

v2x.headline: The Mobility House Unveils New Vehicle-to-Grid Integration Platform for Utilities
v2x.summary: The Mobility House launched Cascade EV Aggregator, an EV load aggregation tool enabling charging and discharging optimization across home chargers and electric school bus fleets.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging (V2G) in 2026: Which Cars Support It?
v2x.summary: EcoFlow analysis shows 2026 as breakthrough year for bidirectional charging with major automakers like Ford, Tesla, and GM offering V2L, V2H, and V2G capabilities across their EV lines.

v2x.headline: BMW Presents First Commercial V2G Offering with E.ON for Germany
v2x.summary: At IAA 2025, BMW announced its first commercial V2G solution for Germany with E.ON, featuring the BMW iX3 and bidirectional BMW Wallbox Professional launching spring 2026.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz Announces Bidirectional Charging for 2026 with GLC Launch
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz announced introduction of bidirectional charging in 2026, with the new all-electric GLC leading the way in their V2G rollout strategy.

v2x.headline: Enphase Slates Volume Production of Bidirectional EV Chargers for Q4 2026
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy plans to begin manufacturing its IQ Bidirectional EV Charging Platform at volume production in Q4 2026 following successful product development and trials.

v2x.headline: Vehicle to Grid (V2G) Technology Market Worth USD 251.16 Bn by 2034
v2x.summary: Global V2G market projected to grow from $15.85 billion in 2025 to $251.16 billion by 2034 at 35.93% CAGR, driven by grid integration and smart technology adoption.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Home V2H Charging: A Practical Guide Using Your EV as Home UPS
v2x.summary: TechTimes explores V2H technology allowing EVs to act as flexible home energy sources, detailing how bidirectional charging provides backup power and cost savings in 2026.

v2x.headline: These Are All The EVs That Can Power Your Home
v2x.summary: InsideEVs reviews V2H-capable vehicles including Ford F-150 Lightning, Nissan Leaf, GM EVs, and Kia models, highlighting available bidirectional charging systems for home backup.

v2x.headline: Kia EV9 Owners Gain Access to V2H Charging with Wallbox Quasar 2
v2x.summary: Wallbox opened pre-orders for Quasar 2 charger with Power Recovery Unit for eligible Kia EV9 owners, enabling V2H capability for backup power and cost savings.

v2x.headline: Grid Impacts of Electric Vehicle Charging: A Review of Challenges and Mitigation Strategies
v2x.summary: Academic review examines EV charging impacts on distribution grids and mitigation strategies including smart charging, renewable integration, and V2G optimization techniques.

v2x.headline: Installing Bidirectional Charging Solutions: Overcoming Complexities
v2x.summary: Qmerit explores technological and installation complexities of bidirectional charging, noting Nissan as first UL 9741 certified and GM's 2026 rollout plans.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Chargers Review - V2G & V2H
v2x.summary: Clean Energy Reviews provides comprehensive analysis of V2G and V2H charger options including Wallbox Quasar, Sigenergy SigenStor, and upcoming Enphase offerings.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Future Trends & Use Cases
v2x.summary: Driivz analysis shows Tesla planning 2025 bidirectional capability and GM 2026 standard rollout, with EV batteries capable of providing 32-62 TWh capacity by 2050.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicle Batteries Could Satisfy Grid Storage Demand by 2030
v2x.summary: Nature Communications research indicates EV batteries alone could meet short-term grid storage needs by 2030 with higher participation rates and utilization levels.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid, Why Not? Social Acceptance and Behavioral Adoption in Utrecht
v2x.summary: ScienceDirect study explores social dimensions of V2G adoption through interviews with BEV users in Utrecht, highlighting acceptance factors beyond technical feasibility.

v2x.headline: Driving Energy Forward: An Introduction to Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G)
v2x.summary: Clean Energy Works explains V2G technology, current utility pilots including National Grid and PG&E, and recommendations for federal and state support to accelerate adoption.

v2x.headline: What Is Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Technology and How Does It Work?
v2x.summary: EV Connect explores V2G fundamentals showing how bidirectional chargers convert AC to DC and back, enabling grid services while facing regulatory fragmentation challenges.

v2x.headline: Cars With Bidirectional Charging You Can Buy In 2025
v2x.summary: CarBuzz lists 10 bidirectional charging EVs available in 2025 including Ford F-150 Lightning, Nissan Leaf, and Hyundai models, with details on power output capabilities.
